purpose: Interval direct construction through the aliased public constructor (pyreason.interval.closed) over four bound classes: a mid overlap, an equal pair differing only in the static flag (equality/hash ignore static and prev), inverted bounds (l>u accepted unvalidated; intersection clamps the empty result to [0,1]), and a disjoint pair (same clamp). Each probe's fixed pipeline also pins the reset transition (prev := current, bounds := [0,1] — reset() itself ignores the static flag; the static guard lives at the engine call site) and the PROXY intersection's prev-bound seeding from self's CURRENT bounds (interval.py:69) via the post-reset intersection — the jitted overload seeds from self's PREV bounds (interval_type.py:63), a two-implementation divergence only reachable by compiling new jitted code, so the jitted arm stays unobserved here. All bound values are exact binary fractions.
provenance: docs/surface.md type:Interval closed-happy/closed-static/inverted-bounds/intersection-empty/reset-semantics/has-changed/contains/equality + the proxy arm of prev-seed-mismatch; interval.py:6-90, interval_type.py:143-145 at the pin; screened live 2026-07-07 (screen_types.py)
